cards.title: Free Will?
cards.body: Philosopher John Locke argued that our identity is based on conscious memory, influencing modern ideas on free will and personal identity.

cards.title: Philosophy of Time
cards.body: Philosopher Henri Poincaré proposed the concept of conventionalism, which posits that time is a human construct, relative and dependent on our measurement tools.

cards.title: Buridan's Donkey
cards.body: In a thought experiment, Jean Buridan's donkey starves between two equal piles of food, illustrating indecision and the problem of arbitrary choice.

cards.title: Hobbes' Social Contract
cards.body: Thomas Hobbes argued that humans are naturally self-interested and need a strong central authority to maintain social order and prevent chaos.

cards.title: Nietzsche's Will
cards.body: Friedrich Nietzsche introduced the concept of the 'will to power,' arguing that human behavior is driven by a fundamental desire for self-overcoming and excellence.

cards.title: Kant's Categorical
cards.body: Immanuel Kant developed the categorical imperative, a moral principle that universalizes individual actions, providing a framework for ethical decision-making.

cards.title: The Euthyphro Dilemma
cards.body: In the Euthyphro dialogue, Plato presents a dilemma: are moral rules based on divine command, or are they independent of divine will?

cards.title: Russell's Teapot
cards.body: Philosopher Bertrand Russell introduced the concept of Russell's teapot, arguing that unfalsifiable claims, like the existence of a celestial teapot, are meaningless.

cards.title: Aristotle's Hylomorphism
cards.body: Aristotle proposed hylomorphism, the idea that reality consists of matter and form, which underlies his metaphysics and philosophy of nature.

cards.title: Mill's Utilitarianism
cards.body: John Stuart Mill developed the theory of utilitarianism, which argues that actions are right if they promote the greatest happiness for the greatest number of people.

quizzes.question: Who argued our identity is based on conscious memory?
quizzes.explanation: John Locke argued that our identity is based on conscious memory, influencing modern ideas on free will and personal identity.

quizzes.question: Who argued humans are naturally self-interested?
quizzes.explanation: Thomas Hobbes argued that humans are naturally self-interested and need a strong central authority to maintain social order and prevent chaos.

quizzes.question: What dilemma presents a moral principle based on divine command?
quizzes.explanation: The Euthyphro dilemma presents a moral principle based on divine command, or independent of divine will.
